Hong Kong Protesters Statement on 9.11 Rumours

The past three months have seen our commitment to the principle of “no isolating, condemning, or snitching” among the protesters, which has successfully unified the “peaceful-rational camp” and the “courageous-militant camp”. Our solidarity, however, is not to be abused, especially when it comes to moral issues.

From the beginning of the anti-extradition bill movement, we have kept the manifestations as non-violent as possible, only to be met with brutal crackdowns by the Hong Kong police, resulting in an unnecessary escalation of violence. While the police and triads have launched numerous indiscriminate attacks on innocent civilians, such terrorist acts are never an option for us.

The rumours about the plot of arson and mass murder by protesters on 9.11 are, therefore, simply not true. Given that the story is “leaked” by China Daily, and that Chinese Communist Party and the Hong Kong government have helped disseminate a tremendous amount of fake news to defame and disintegrate the democratic movement, it is highly suspected of being another propaganda move to discourage the local and international communities from supporting us.

We hereby condemn any sort of terrorism, and urge the public not to play into the hands of the plotters. Humanitarian values are crucial to our revolution, which, for this very reason, has been going strong for months, and stronger in the future.
